#add_case_insights(case_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Object

Add insights to a case.

  • #add_case_insights_with_http_info

# File 'lib/datadog_api_client/v2/api/case_management_api.rb', line 29

def add_case_insights(case_id, body, opts = {})
  data, _status_code, _headers = add_case_insights_with_http_info(case_id, body, opts)
  data
end

#add_case_insights_with_http_info(case_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Add insights to a case.

Adds one or more insights to a case. Insights are references to related Datadog resources (such as monitors, security signals, incidents, or error tracking issues) that provide investigative context. Up to 100 insights can be added per request. Each insight requires a type (see CaseInsightType for allowed values), a ref (URL path to the resource), and a resource_id.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

  • body (CaseInsightsRequest) —

    Case insights request.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(CaseResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    CaseResponse data, response status code and response headers

def add_case_insights_with_http_info(case_id, body, opts = {})
  unstable_enabled = @api_client.config.unstable_operations["v2.add_case_insights".to_sym]
  if unstable_enabled
    @api_client.config.logger.warn format("Using unstable operation '%s'", "v2.add_case_insights")
  else
    raise DatadogAPIClient::APIError.new(message: format("Unstable operation '%s' is disabled", "v2.add_case_insights"))
  end

  if @api_client.config.debugging
    @api_client.config.logger.debug 'Calling API: CaseManagementAPI.add_case_insights ...'
  end
  # verify the required parameter 'case_id' is set
  if @api_client.config.client_side_validation && case_id.nil?
    fail ArgumentError, "Missing the required parameter 'case_id' when calling CaseManagementAPI.add_case_insights"
  end
  # verify the required parameter 'body' is set
  if @api_client.config.client_side_validation && body.nil?
    fail ArgumentError, "Missing the required parameter 'body' when calling CaseManagementAPI.add_case_insights"
  end
  # resource path
  local_var_path = '/api/v2/cases/{case_id}/insights'.sub('{case_id}', CGI.escape(case_id.to_s).gsub('%2F', '/'))

  # query parameters
  query_params = opts[:query_params] || {}

  # header parameters
  header_params = opts[:header_params] || {}
  # HTTP header 'Accept' (if needed)
  header_params['Accept'] = @api_client.select_header_accept(['application/json'])
  # HTTP header 'Content-Type'
  header_params['Content-Type'] = @api_client.select_header_content_type(['application/json'])

  # form parameters
  form_params = opts[:form_params] || {}

  # http body (model)
  post_body = opts[:debug_body] || @api_client.object_to_http_body(body)

  # return_type
  return_type = opts[:debug_return_type] || 'CaseResponse'

  # auth_names
  auth_names = opts[:debug_auth_names] || [:apiKeyAuth, :appKeyAuth, :AuthZ]

  new_options = opts.merge(
    :operation => :add_case_insights,
    :header_params => header_params,
    :query_params => query_params,
    :form_params => form_params,
    :body => post_body,
    :auth_names => auth_names,
    :return_type => return_type,
    :api_version => "V2"
  )

  data, status_code, headers = @api_client.call_api(Net::HTTP::Put, local_var_path, new_options)
  if @api_client.config.debugging
    @api_client.config.logger.debug "API called: CaseManagementAPI#add_case_insights\nData: #{data.inspect}\nStatus code: #{status_code}\nHeaders: #{headers}"
  end
  return data, status_code, headers
end

def aggregate_cases(body, opts = {})
  data, _status_code, _headers = aggregate_cases_with_http_info(body, opts)
  data
end

#aggregate_cases_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseAggregateResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Aggregate cases.

Performs an aggregation query over cases, grouping results by specified fields and returning counts per group along with a total. Useful for dashboards and analytics.

Parameters:

  • body (CaseAggregateRequest) —

    Case aggregate request payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(CaseAggregateResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    CaseAggregateResponse data, response status code and response headers

#bulk_update_cases_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Bulk update cases.

Applies a single action (such as changing priority, status, assignment, or archiving) to multiple cases at once. The list of case IDs and the action type with its payload are specified in the request body.

Parameters:

  • body (CaseBulkUpdateRequest) —

    Case bulk update request payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>) —

    nil, response status code and response headers

#count_cases_with_http_info(opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseCountResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Count cases.

Returns case counts, optionally grouped by one or more fields (for example, status, priority). Supports a query filter to narrow the scope.

Parameters:

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :query_filter (String) —

    Filter query for cases.

  • :group_bys (String) —

    Comma-separated fields to group by.

  • :limit (Integer) —

    Maximum facet values to return.

Returns:

  • (Array<(CaseCountResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    CaseCountResponse data, response status code and response headers

Create an automation rule.

Creates an automation rule for a project. The rule defines a trigger event (for example, case created, status transitioned) and an action to execute.

    The UUID of the project that owns the automation rules.

#create_case_link_with_http_info(body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseLinkResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Create a case link.

Creates a directional link between two cases (for example, case A blocks case B). The parent and child cases and their relationship type must be specified.

Parameters:

  • body (CaseLinkCreateRequest) —

    Case link create request.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

#disable_case_automation_rule_with_http_info(project_id, rule_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(AutomationRuleResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Disable an automation rule.

Disables an automation rule so it no longer triggers on case events. The rule configuration is preserved.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The UUID of the project that owns the automation rules.

  • rule_id (String) —

    The UUID of the automation rule.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(AutomationRuleResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

#list_case_automation_rules_with_http_info(project_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(AutomationRulesResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List automation rules.

Returns all automation rules configured for a project. Automation rules allow automatic actions to be triggered by case events like creation, status transitions, or attribute changes.

Parameters:

  • project_id (String) —

    The UUID of the project that owns the automation rules.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(AutomationRulesResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    AutomationRulesResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_case_links_with_http_info(entity_type, entity_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseLinksResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List case links.

Returns all links associated with a case. Links define relationships (for example, BLOCKS) between cases. Requires entity_type and entity_id query parameters.

Parameters:

  • entity_type (String) —

    The entity type to look up links for. Use CASE to find links for a specific case.

  • entity_id (String) —

    The UUID of the entity to look up links for.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :relationship (String) —

    Optional filter to only return links of a specific relationship type (for example, BLOCKS or CAUSES).

Returns:

  • (Array<(CaseLinksResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    CaseLinksResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_case_timeline_with_http_info(case_id, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(TimelineResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Get case timeline.

Returns the timeline of events for a case, including comments, status changes, and other activity. Supports pagination and sort order.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Options Hash (opts):

  • :page_size (Integer) —

    Number of timeline cells to return per page.

  • :page_number (Integer) —

    Zero-based page number for pagination.

  • :sort_ascending (Boolean) —

    If true, returns timeline cells in chronological order (oldest first). Defaults to false (newest first).

Returns:

  • (Array<(TimelineResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    TimelineResponse data, response status code and response headers

#list_maintenance_windows_with_http_info(opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(MaintenanceWindowsResponse, Integer, Hash)>

List maintenance windows.

Returns all configured maintenance windows for event management cases. Maintenance windows define time periods during which case notifications and automation rules are suppressed for cases matching a given query.

Parameters:

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(MaintenanceWindowsResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

Search cases.

Options Hash (opts):

    Size for a given page. The maximum allowed value is 100.

  • :page_number (Integer) —

    Specific page number to return.

    Search query

# File 'lib/datadog_api_client/v2/api/case_management_api.rb', line 3298

def search_cases_with_http_info(opts = {})

  if @api_client.config.debugging
    @api_client.config.logger.debug 'Calling API: CaseManagementAPI.search_cases ...'
  end
  allowable_values = ['created_at', 'priority', 'status']
  if @api_client.config.client_side_validation && opts[:'sort_field'] && !allowable_values.include?(opts[:'sort_field'])
    fail ArgumentError, "invalid value for \"sort_field\", must be one of #{allowable_values}"
  end
  # resource path
  local_var_path = '/api/v2/cases'

  # query parameters
  query_params = opts[:query_params] || {}
  query_params[:'page[size]'] = opts[:'page_size'] if !opts[:'page_size'].nil?
  query_params[:'page[number]'] = opts[:'page_number'] if !opts[:'page_number'].nil?
  query_params[:'sort[field]'] = opts[:'sort_field'] if !opts[:'sort_field'].nil?
  query_params[:'filter'] = opts[:'filter'] if !opts[:'filter'].nil?
  query_params[:'sort[asc]'] = opts[:'sort_asc'] if !opts[:'sort_asc'].nil?

  # header parameters
  header_params = opts[:header_params] || {}
  # HTTP header 'Accept' (if needed)
  header_params['Accept'] = @api_client.select_header_accept(['application/json'])

  # form parameters
  form_params = opts[:form_params] || {}

  # http body (model)
  post_body = opts[:debug_body]

  # return_type
  return_type = opts[:debug_return_type] || 'CasesResponse'

  # auth_names
  auth_names = opts[:debug_auth_names] || [:apiKeyAuth, :appKeyAuth, :AuthZ]

  new_options = opts.merge(
    :operation => :search_cases,
    :header_params => header_params,
    :query_params => query_params,
    :form_params => form_params,
    :body => post_body,
    :auth_names => auth_names,
    :return_type => return_type,
    :api_version => "V2"
  )

  data, status_code, headers = @api_client.call_api(Net::HTTP::Get, local_var_path, new_options)
  if @api_client.config.debugging
    @api_client.config.logger.debug "API called: CaseManagementAPI#search_cases\nData: #{data.inspect}\nStatus code: #{status_code}\nHeaders: #{headers}"
  end
  return data, status_code, headers
end

#search_cases_with_pagination(opts = {}) {|Case| ... } ⇒ Object

Search cases.

Provide a paginated version of #search_cases, returning all items.

To use it you need to use a block: search_cases_with_pagination { |item| p item }

Yields:

  • (Case) —

    Paginated items



3360
3361
3362
3363
3364
3365
3366
3367
3368
3369
3370
3371
3372
3373
 
# File 'lib/datadog_api_client/v2/api/case_management_api.rb', line 3360

def search_cases_with_pagination(opts = {})
    api_version = "V2"
    page_size = @api_client.get_attribute_from_path(opts, "page_size", 10)
    @api_client.set_attribute_from_path(api_version, opts, "page_size", Integer, page_size)
    @api_client.set_attribute_from_path(api_version, opts, "page_number", Integer, 1)
    while true do
        response = search_cases(opts)
        @api_client.get_attribute_from_path(response, "data").each { |item| yield(item) }
        if @api_client.get_attribute_from_path(response, "data").length < page_size
          break
        end
        @api_client.set_attribute_from_path(api_version, opts, "page_number", Integer, @api_client.get_attribute_from_path(opts, "page_number", 1) + 1)
    end
end

#unwatch_case_with_http_info(case_id, user_uuid, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Unwatch a case.

Removes a user from the watchers list of a case. The user no longer receives notifications about updates to the case.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

#update_case_comment_with_http_info(case_id, cell_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Update case comment.

Updates the text content of an existing comment on a case timeline. The comment is identified by its cell ID.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

  • cell_id (String) —

    The UUID of the timeline cell (comment) to update.

  • body (CaseUpdateCommentRequest) —

    Case update comment payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>) —

    nil, response status code and response headers

#update_case_due_date_with_http_info(case_id, body, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(CaseResponse, Integer, Hash)>

Update case due date.

Sets or updates the due date for a case. The due date is a calendar date (without a time component) indicating when the case should be resolved.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

  • body (CaseUpdateDueDateRequest) —

    Case due date update payload.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(CaseResponse, Integer, Hash)>) —

    CaseResponse data, response status code and response headers

#watch_case_with_http_info(case_id, user_uuid, opts = {}) ⇒ Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>

Watch a case.

Adds a user (identified by their UUID) as a watcher of a case. The user receives notifications about subsequent updates to the case.

Parameters:

  • case_id (String) —

    Case's UUID or key

  • user_uuid (String) —

    The UUID of the user to add or remove as a watcher.

  • opts (Hash) (defaults to: {}) —

    the optional parameters

Returns:

  • (Array<(nil, Integer, Hash)>) —

    nil, response status code and response headers
